title: olea aquifolium var. subintegra
intro: Olea aquifolium var. subintegra, also known as the Holly Olive, is a plant species within the Oleaceae family, characterized by its shrub-like growth and waxy leaves. The medicinal properties of the Holly Olive plant are attributed to its essential oils, which contain bioactive compounds such as oleuropein and oleanolic acid, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of the Holly Olive plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and antiviral effects. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
